transcript.text: Hey, good morning.
This meeting is being recorded.
Yeah, doing great over here.
Just kind of had a relaxing weekend.
How's it going, Rich?
Good to see you.
Good.
How are you?
I'm good.
I'm good.
Well, today should be pretty easy.

Really just wanted to make some time to walk through, you know, any questions that you might have around the partnership, schedule our kickoff call for next week, and just talk a little bit about the logistics for how we would create and review content.
But yeah, I guess I can see Nima and Rich if you have any questions you'd like to talk through.
Yeah, let me get started maybe.
First of all, congrats, being able to jump through so many hoops.
We finally are here.
Do you know where you landed on the laptops and access?
Yes, so that I make sure that if you need those ones, I need to take care of those.
Yeah, Mithil said that we would not need.
We to use separate laptops, and we talked about that, I would say, quite a while ago, so we should be good to have our team use our laptops.
Okay, cool.
Okay, so trying to figure out what's the right approach, how do you usually work with the teams?
What the kickoff is needed for?
We could share with you how we usually send the content for approval, but before we go that, what is your workflow?
How do you usually hand it over so that we better understand that?
Yeah, definitely.

So our workflow, we would typically create like a Google Doc that has the draft for the article, and then there's a lot of scenarios where we would access the CMS and stage and publish on your behalf.
But I know in this scenario, we may just work with someone who, I guess, has that.
Access to the CMS and, you know, I'm assuming that we're probably going to go that route so that they could basically take our draft and stage and publish.
What's our process at the moment, Rich, if they give us, because I don't know, first of all, we're able to get them access to CMS and if we do, we know how complicated our CMS is.
Is that something that they, if they provide it to us, we are able to do it or what's your take on that?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Production of a post, we're talking about, we're talking about standard blog post productions, pretty straightforward.
So that's, that's very quick.
But the big question right now is we're going through kind of a change with, Nima had mentioned our approvals process and we're going through kind of a change with that.
So, um,
Just because legal needs to review everything moving forward.
So we've got a couple email aliases right now that we send through to approvals, but that is in the process of changing.

And I'm not sure exactly how that's going to work yet, but it'll be really, I think, just once we have copies, sending it through to whatever alias or aliases we end up using so that proofers can prove it, legal can look at it, investor relations can look at it before, you know, and just making sure we build in, we bake in time for that process to happen moving forward.
So, but production wise, I'm not, I'm not too concerned about, unless we're talking about high volume.
And of content, it's pretty quick to produce a story.
Yeah, it would be probably three to four a week, I assume, at some point.
I don't know, Tyler, probably we start a little slower.
I assume the beginning is going to be more research, and then we ramp up.
Is that the correct assumption?
That's correct.
The first two weeks, we'll have our kickoff call, where we'll schedule a product deep dive with some folks from your product marketing org.
We'll generate the artifacts, and then we'll start to set up the content calendar and calibration articles.
So we would look to start publishing our first article probably around week five of the engagement.
I think the key is, you know, Rich, typically, it's the biggest bottleneck is when there are several points of review.
If we're creating more SEO, GEO content that isn't referencing Navon, would we still have to have legal and...

The investor relations team review every single article because in that scenario it would be like you or Libby has the initial review of the copy and then it would go to two different teams that don't have a specific point of contact but are email aliases.
And I feel like if that's the case for every article, I don't know what quantity we could get to.
So, yes, everything has to go to legal, but why does that matter?
You produce it and then we just launch it as they come, right?
What's the concern there?
I just think if there are three different rounds of reviews and there are so many different people giving feedback, then there's probably going to be a lot of revisions.
And if we're targeting five articles per week, I just can't see how we would be able to get five completed.
For the type of content that you receive, I don't think, you're right, I don't think, unless you start talking about the product specifically, product feature.
Then the legal review, I assume, is going to be very minimal because if you're not saying anything that we cannot substantiate, then they don't revise it, right?
So the only review would be through Rich or Libby on our side.
And then once it's reviewed, then we could take care of the legal aspect of it.
We might not be able to release everything at the same time, but we could kind of send them batches and get that for walls and then build those mozzanagland bases.
Yeah, just to follow up, we haven't seen any pushback from legal except in the case where we're making claims about the product.
So if we're talking very general, I'm not expecting, you know, it would be minimal if any changes.
So, okay, perfect.
So what we'll do is we'll have our batches of our...
Articles, Rich or Libby, you can basically give feedback and approve from the Google Doc or from our Slack channel.
And then you'll be able to send approved articles from your side to the legal and investor relations teams.
And because we won't be making claims about the product, should be a quick review to be able to move into publishing.
Would we work with Moran, Nima, on the staging and publishing?
No, he runs our SEO.
So I wanted to make sure that he's part of the conversation, given the fact that this project has an impact on SEO.

So we might kind of, the way Moran and I are going to involve with you are going to be around what keywords to optimize for and making sure that we have the right cross-linking strategy there, but what the content-wise is going to be.
Rich, and maybe that'll help you.
And then the only thing is that we have to decide where we want this content to be hosted.
So I probably want to have a dedicated URL structure for this type of content that we're producing with you so that we'll be able to track the impact separately.
We need to come up with a naming convention for it.
Rich, for that, and that could be in a form of a subdirectory and a blog.
Or we could get even more sophisticated and create pillar pages.
That one, we need to work with Nier, or is that something that you have ability to build within the current blog structure?
That's a great question.
I'm not sure.
We'll have to look into it.
Okay, we could check on that one.
But Tyler, for you, how do you usually work with the teams?
Do they build pillar pages?
Do they just create random blog pages?
How do
Do they organize the content?
Yeah, it would typically be publishing to the blog.
Maybe there's a different category that we'd publish to, but typically just in the existing blog section of the site.
Okay.
So I'll start a thread with our web team to see what their take is on it, and if we need their support there, or it's an easy one that we're going to build on our site.
Sounds good.

Now, the other thing that you mentioned that you said that you want to have an interview sessions with the product marketing team, what do you want to get out of that if you already have the artifacts around the personas and messaging, and I love that.
Just trying to make sure that they're really stretched in, so I just want to make sure that we are using the time properly.
Yeah, absolutely.
That could be, like, 30-minute call, and it could just be with, like, one or two representatives from the team, but the point
The point of having that call is to capture the nuance that the product experts would have that we're not going to capture through ingesting resources or generating artifacts.
It's really just to get some of the finer details about like what makes your product special.
And typically, you know, that call is really helpful for the team just to pick the brain, right, of like the people closest to the product.
Okay, so do you need that after you receive the content or before?
It would be after the kickoff call.
So two things that we could schedule today, maybe just one, so you can identify like who the right person is, would be the kickoff call for Monday the 17th.
Just making sure we can have, you know, you, Nima, and then Rich and Libby, and then I think Maran would also be helpful to include.
So that would be all we would need for the kickoff call.
And then for the product deep dive, that could be a 30-minute follow-up.
Maybe, you know, the same week, so next week, with two people from the product marketing team, one or two.
Okay.
What time are we able to make it happen?
On Monday, you said 17?
Yeah, and we can be flexible if like Tuesday the 18th is better for you.
But on Monday, I know you mentioned like earlier could be better for the folks in Israel.
We could do 8 a.m.
Pacific on Monday the 17th.
I have conflicts at 8 a.m., maybe 8.30.
Yeah, 8.30 definitely works.
We could do that.
How much time do we need?
45 minutes would be great for our kickoff.
45 minutes.
Yeah.
Or even an hour if possible.
Sorry, you said you need the product marketing on that call as well?
Okay.
you.
No, no.
For this one, this will just be the core team.
So Rich and Libby, Maran, yourself.
For the product deep dive, we can do 30 minutes and it can be with one or two people from product marketing.
Okay, cool.
What time on the 17th or 18th is best for the core team?
Do you think 8.30 works for you, Rich?
You're 11.30.
Yes, for sure.
Okay, cool.
Let me find a time for us, block, and I add it as well.
And then feel free to add anybody else that you think is needed on your side, Tyler, as well.
Okay, perfect.
Are you sending the invite to us or do you want me to send it over to you?
I'm just sending it right there as we speak.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, Mohan might not be able to join, but his calendar is back after 7 a.m., so I don't know.
We could move forward if we can't join it.
That sounds good.
And then, Nima, is the team able to join our Slack channel?
That's typically an easy place for us to communicate and share resources.
Yeah, do you mind resending the invite to everybody on the calendar since you – we'll see if the one that you have sent, unfortunately, got expired.
We need to bring the new one.
Okay, sounds good.
I'll send this to the folks in the calendar invite, but I also just threw it in Slack if you want to see if it's working now.
Okay, let me me
Who can see us selected as private for now?
That's fine, right?
Yeah, that's fine.
The external Navan, then I would have difficulty remembering it's you guys.
Okay.

And then I think if there are any resources that you guys could share, especially brand guidelines, any sort of like product overviews or, you know, ICP resources, positioning, that definitely helps the team just accelerate the process.
But I know there's like...
Some requirements around security, so we did sign the addendum, so that's in place.
But yeah, anything that you guys are able to share related to the brand, the product, or your ICP would be super helpful for our team.
Okay.
So how do I access that Slack?
Let's see if it worked.
Do you need to approve it, or?
It might be on your end.
We don't need to approve it, but...
It might on my side, yeah.
Okay.
Let's see what happens after...
Uh...
Yeah, I made the request to create it, but I don't see it.
Yeah, no worries.
It's probably on your side, but we can work it out over the course of the next week.
Richard, are there any resources that come to mind that you might be able to share?
We do have a brand hub or anything.
We do have some brand guidelines on our marketing hub.
We also have a product playbook that the PMMs have put together that should be able to, that would be helpful.
They're kind of a messaging guide.
Yeah, we probably want to have messaging guide, cue cards, as well as the brand guidelines.
playbook that have have playbook use.
Phone and voice, all of that.
I could share it or, Rich, you feel like you have better access to them.
But I think I could do it as well.
Okay, yeah, I can pull the brand guidelines and the messaging playbooks.
That is cool.
Yeah, I think we need to give them the cue card as well.
Where are those these days?
Oh, okay.
Don't worry.
I'll take care of it.
Okay.
Yeah.
Once we have the Slack channel, we could share it with you.
But in the meantime, I'll it with you, Rich, so you have a handy.
And Nima, this email address right there, team at growthxlabs.com.
That's the best one to share.

There was a lot of back and forth, so I have to connect with them, see where they landed, because they told me that you need a laptop, and Mithil tells you that you don't need one, so you have to figure that one.
They definitely said no to Amplitube.
They were open to Google, but I don't know where they landed on that one.
Okay, no worries.

Well, I'll send an email that recaps some of the next steps we discussed, but we've got our kickoff call on the calendar, and Nima, whenever you identify 30 minutes that work well for the product Deep Dive with one person from that team, we can schedule that for the same week, maybe a few days after.
Rich, what's your top?
Kida would be able to talk to Expense as well, or he would be only focusing on travel?
Yeah, mostly travel.
Okay, so we have two different product lines with two different product marketers, and Marie is going on maternity leave, right?
Do you know what the timing is for her?
I don't.
Let's see if she has her calendar up to wait, because we might be losing her in the short...
Yeah, soon.
It's coming up.
forget exactly when.
see where her calendar is.
We could do two separate meetings if you think it's important, whatever works out.
I think we need to do that, but the question I have is when she's going to head out to maternity leave.
So I just want...
we might have to accelerate it a little bit.
Let me see.
I think...
okay, so she has...
think not speak...
It's clocked starting 28th, it seems.
So we should have, oh, no, that's just one day.
So she hasn't updated her calendar.
Anyway, I'll schedule something if we can't.
But let me start with Nikita first.
And then, do you know when Nikita is coming to US?
Rich, did he tell you?
That's a good point.
It's also very soon.
And I think that he's coming this week.
So then we could find a US time zone as well.
That's what it is.
Okay.
Let me find us the time for us for him as well.
What's your schedule on the, you said we need 30 minutes, right?
30 minutes is fine.
30 minutes, okay.
So let's aim for maybe Thursday 20th.
Yeah, that works great.
Does your 10.30 Pacific time work?
Let's see.
Let's Let's
And do you need Rich for that?
Rich and Libby or not?
Just the product marketer, but Rich or Libby is welcome to join.
I've got 10 a.m.
and 11.30 a.m.
Pacific.
Would either of those work?
Pacific?
Yes.
10 and what again?
11.30 or 1 to 3 p.m.
Pacific.
Just to make sure that he's, I don't know.
Or even 12.30, yeah.
Let's try 10 a.m.
Okay, 10 a.m.
And do you want to send that invite as well, Nima?
I will send it.
Yeah, perfect.
Perfect.
Well, we're looking forward to kicking off, and I know this has been a long time coming, but Rich, you'll have a lot of help from GrowthX when it comes to your SEO, GEO.
Yeah.
Content initiatives and our team will take any resources that you share and put some prep in ahead of the kickoff next Monday.
Do you mind sending an agenda about the kickoff so that I could add to the calendars for both the content team as well as the PMM teams?
Absolutely.
I'll be sure to include that in my follow-up email and I'll make sure I send that before I log off today.
Okay.
Sounds like a plan.
All right.
Thanks, gentlemen.
Talk soon.
Thanks, Tyler.
See you.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize kickoff logistics and content workflow for the GrowthX partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=15.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Kickoff Scheduled:** The kickoff is set for Mon, Nov 17 at 8:30 AM PT. Product deep-dives with PMMs (Nikita, Marie) will follow to capture product nuance.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1185.0)
  - [**Content Workflow Defined:** GrowthX drafts content in Google Docs → Navan (Rich/Libby) approves → Navan sends to Legal/IR for final review.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=72.0)
  - [**Review Bottleneck Mitigated:** The new Legal/IR review process is not a blocker. General SEO content (not making product claims) requires minimal review, enabling the target volume of 3–4 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=282.0)
  - [**Tracking Strategy:** A dedicated URL structure (e.g., subdirectory) will be created to isolate and track the performance of GrowthX-generated content.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

## Topics

### Content Workflow & Approvals

  - [**GrowthX Process:** Drafts in Google Docs; Navan handles staging/publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
  - [**Navan's New Approval Process:** All content now requires review from Legal and Investor Relations (IR) via email aliases.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=169.0)
  - [**Resolution:** This is not a blocker for the target volume of 3–4 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**Rationale:** General SEO content (not making product claims) requires minimal review from Legal/IR.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=350.0)
      - [**Workflow:** GrowthX drafts → Rich/Libby approve → Navan sends to Legal/IR for final review.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=415.0)

### Content Strategy & Tracking

  - [**Goal:** Isolate the performance of GrowthX content for clear impact tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**Method:** Create a dedicated URL structure, such as a subdirectory on the main blog.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**Action:** Nima will consult the web team on implementation.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**SEO Collaboration:** Moran (Navan SEO) will advise on keyword strategy and cross-linking.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)

### Kickoff & Onboarding

  - [**Kickoff Call:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
      - [**Date/Time:** Mon, Nov 17 @ 8:30 AM PT (45–60 min)](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
      - [**Attendees:** Nima, Rich, Libby, Moran (optional)](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=634.0)
  - [**Product Deep-Dives:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=566.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Capture product nuance from experts not found in documentation.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=566.0)
      - [**Travel Product:** 30-min call with Nikita (PMM) scheduled for Thu, Nov 20 @ 10:00 AM PT.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1215.0)
      - [**Expense Product:** A separate call will be scheduled with Marie (PMM) before her maternity leave (starts \~Nov 28).](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1149.0)
  - [**Resource Sharing:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=876.0)
      - [**Owner:** Rich will provide brand guidelines and the product playbook.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
      - [**Owner:** Nima will provide cue cards.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1012.0)
      - [**Recipient:** `team@growthxlabs.com`](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1067.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1328.0)
      - [Send kickoff agenda to all attendees.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1328.0)
      - [Resend Slack channel invite to the core team.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=795.0)
  - [**Nima:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1149.0)
      - [Schedule the 30-min product deep-dive with Marie (Expense PMM).](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1149.0)
      - [Consult the web team on creating a dedicated URL structure.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [Share cue cards with Rich.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=1012.0)
  - [**Rich:**](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
      - [Share brand guidelines and the product playbook with `team@growthxlabs.com`.](https://fathom.video/share/JK6v8xwc3EBVRyUCMgn1sKN_oHLqH5yE?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)

action_items.description: Start thread w/ web team re: dedicated URL structure + naming; decide CMS approach
Resend Slack invite to Nima + kickoff attendees; confirm access
Email brand guidelines, messaging playbook, cue cards to Tyler (team@growthxlabs.com)
Confirm w/ security/IT laptop/access decision; update Tyler
Email Nima kickoff agenda + recap (cc Rich, Libby, Moran)
Schedule 30-min PMM deep dive w/ Marie before Nov 28
Send 30-min PMM deep dive invite to Nikita (Thu Nov 20 10:00 PT)
